mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, discount-rate pressure eases and equity multiples expand, so S&P 500 forward returns are positive over the next 20 trading days. The signal is directly tied to the monthly rate path and fails when the policy rate is flat or rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and demand, which reduces near-term equity earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when unemployment accelerates rather than remains stable.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show below-zero average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broadening nominal demand and labor income, which supports corporate sales growth and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal depends on both consumption and payroll breadth, not either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS growth and PAYEMS growth does not outperform each series individually in rank IC, or if the combined signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in INDPRO below trend marks an industrial production shock that compresses earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next 5 trading days. The mechanism is a short-lag risk-off response to weaker real activity.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day SPX returns are not negative on average after the trigger, or if the triggered win rate is indistinguishable from 50% over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST signals collapsing housing demand and future consumer durables activity, which weakens cyclical earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is a medium-lag slowdown channel rather than an immediate market reaction.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not lower when HOUST trends down for 2 consecutive months, or if the signal has negative out-of-sample rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains very low, real policy rates stay deeply negative and financial conditions are stimulative, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is active only when inflation exceeds the policy rate backdrop by a wide margin.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation > FEDFUNDS by 2.0pp on a trailing 3-month annualized basis
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction does not outperform CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone, or if triggered periods fail to show positive average 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A strong labor-market deterioration signaled by rising UNRATE together with slowing PAYEMS implies recession risk and negative equity repricing over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both fewer jobs and a higher unemployment rate to avoid false positives from one-off payroll noise.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or if deterioration in only one proxy explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, improving RSAFS and GDPC1 indicate that real demand is re-accelerating after a contraction, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if GDP is still contracting or retail sales are not recovering together.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not positive when both GDPC1 and RSAFS improve, or if the relationship disappears when GDP remains negative year-over-year.
